channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Royals' Jonathan India hit by 99 mph fastball, leaves game
item.description: "He threw a pitch that he doesn't ever throw, the pitch before, which was a four-seam fastball. It was his first one of the year," Pasquantino said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, NewsNation

item.title: Authorities investigate damaging fire at New Mexico's GOP headquarters
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, New Mexico (AP) — Federal authorities are investigating a fire early Sunday that damaged the entryway to the headquarters of the Republican Party of New Mexico in Albuquerque.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Historic tree to be cut down at the White House over safety concerns
item.description: A tree that's nearly two centuries old will be removed from the White House grounds because it's deteriorating, President Donald Trump said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Figure skater who lost both parents in DC plane crash brings world championships crowd to its feet
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Maxim Naumov came to a stop in the middle of the ice, looked up at the sky and patted his heart. Then he mouthed a few words, in Russian, to his parents: “This is for you guys. You guys are with me. I love you both.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico taps UC San Diego's Eric Olen to replace Richard Pitino
item.description: New Mexico hired UC San Diego's Eric Olen on Sunday as its next basketball coach. Olen will replace Richard Pitino, who left to become Xavier's coach on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk hands out $1 million payments after Wisconsin Supreme Court declines request to stop him
item.description: Elon Musk has given out $1 million checks to two Wisconsin voters, declaring them spokespeople for his political group.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump roars down multiple paths of retribution as he vowed. Some targets yield while others fight
item.description: In the early part of his second term, President Donald Trump has taken action against individuals, law firms, private corporations, media companies and universities he regards as adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Calvin Woodward, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge blocks Oregon city at center of SCOTUS homelessness ruling from enforcing ban on encampments
item.description: An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction blocking a city from enforcing homeless encampment restrictions unless certain conditions are met.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas man calls 911 after injuring himself during alleged car dealership break-in
item.description: Police in Tyler, Texas, said a man who broke into a car dealership called police on himself when he was injured during the attempted burglary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said. Delta Air Lines Flight 2983 was cleared for takeoff at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport on Friday around 3:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: Democrat mayor arrested for DUI seen on bodycam demanding officers call police chief
item.description: Police found an open container of alcohol in Lumberton Township, NJ Mayor Gina LaPlaca's car while arresting her for DUI, body camera footage shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: State Department sends Congress letter saying it's taking over USAID
item.description: A source tells NewsNation a team trying to organize support for victims of the Myanmar earthquake was told to leave the office at midday.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, pushing past opposition from dentists and national health organizations who warn the move will lead to medical problems that disproportionately affect low-income communities. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about potential worsening inflation and the U.S. economy slowing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: Investigators at the medical examiner's office do not suspect any foul play, a representative told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's anti-DEI executive orders
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A federal judge has temporarily blocked the U.S. Department of Labor from implementing parts of President Donald Trump’s executive orders aimed at curbing diversity, equity and inclusion efforts among federal contractors and grant recipients. Judge Matthew Kennelly of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE SAVAGE and ALEXANDRA OLSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Texas city almost entirely underwater amid rain storms
item.description: Nearly all of the city of Rio Hondo is underwater after the massive rain storms that hit the Rio Grande Valley on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Diana Eva Maldonado, NewsNation

item.title: Jury from outside of Memphis will hear the case of 3 former officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A jury from outside of Memphis will be seated in the state court trial of three former police officers charged with second-degree murder in the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023, a judge ruled Friday. Shelby County Judge James Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and painting the word “resist” on the service center's doors has been arrested, police announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas hospital treating children with vitamin A toxicity: Reports
item.description: The CDC notes that it is not a substitute for vaccination, and warns that overusing Vitamin A can lead to toxicity.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Snake covered by Endangered Species Act killed at Michigan park
item.description: Officials are urging “understanding” over “fear” after a federally protected snake was recently found killed at the Matthaei Botanical Gardens and Nichols Arboretum in Ann Arbor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Consumer sentiment falls to lowest level in over two years
item.description: The University of Michigan's Survey of Consumers found a nearly 12% drop in consumer sentiment to 57 in March.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4
